text.title: python-code-quality Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-code-quality as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Modifying existing code for new requirements instead of extending via composition or strategy patterns.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When PaymentProcessor needs to support a new payment method (Stripe), adding a new elif in process_payment violates Open/Closed. Every new method requires editing the existing class.

failure-mode.description: Ruff auto-fix can be lossy. ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes on B008 (mutable default arg) or UP rewrites can change runtime behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always run tests after unsafe fixes to verify behavior is preserved.

failure-mode.description: mypy + Django dynamic patterns. ORM objects.filter(**kwargs), ForeignKey reverse accessors, request.user typing are weak without django-stubs/mypy-django-plugin. Strict-mode breaks badly without them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Install django-stubs and mypy-django-plugin before enabling strict mode on Django projects.

failure-mode.description: pyright vs mypy disagreement. Same code, different verdicts on Optional narrowing, generics, and TypedDict partials.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pick one for CI; using both creates noise. Pyright is faster for IDE integration; mypy is more configurable for legacy code.

failure-mode.description: # type: ignore rot. Once an agent adds a few, the count compounds. Audits are needed to drive the count down.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run mypy --warn-unused-ignores in CI to audit accumulation. Require justification comments for each ignore.

failure-mode.description: bandit false positives on assert in tests, subprocess with constructed args, MD5 for non-crypto hashing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Suppress per-line with # nosec B### and a justification — not bulk-disable.

failure-mode.description: pre-commit tool version drift. Tool versions in .pre-commit-config.yaml and pyproject.toml must match; otherwise contributors and CI report different errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin versions explicitly in both files. Use pre-commit autoupdate to keep them in sync, but review diffs before committing updates.

failure-mode.description: Coverage gaming. pytest-cov % is local optimum; agents add tests that touch lines without asserting behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Combine with mutation testing (mutmut) for real signal. Branch coverage (not line coverage) is harder to game.

failure-mode.description: SOLID/clean-code "smells" are fuzzy; LLMs over-refactor. Treating structural advice as mechanical fixes is risky.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use SOLID principles as guidance, not law. Not every scenario fits a textbook pattern. Human judgment is required.

failure-mode.description: Agents adding tests that pass but don't validate behavior (test smell: assertion-free tests).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require explicit assertions per test. Use pytest-check or similar to catch tests with no assertions. Code review must validate test quality, not just coverage %.

failure-mode.description: Subclass that violates the Liskov Substitution Principle: accepts different inputs or produces incompatible outputs compared to its parent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When code treats a subclass as its parent type, LSP violations cause runtime surprises. A subclass must accept the same inputs and produce compatible outputs.

failure-mode.description: Large interface that requires implementers to provide methods they don't need, forcing stub implementations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large ABCs violate Interface Segregation. A class implementing TimerListener but only caring about on_tick() is forced to stub on_error() and on_cancel().
